sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute annual purchase orders
and any amendments with L.N. Curtis and Sons for the purchases of:
 Turnouts, and personal protective equipment (PPE) such as wildland coats and
pants, gloves, helmets, hearing and eye protection, and fire boots
 Firefighting tools and equipment such as firehose, fire extinguishers, foam,
ladders, pumps, and related tools and equipment
The purchase order for FY2026 shall not exceed $330,000, with an annual increase no
more than 5% each year through March 27, 2028. A 20% contingency is added to cover
costs arising from unforeseen incidents or operations for a total amount not to exceed
(NTE) $1,040,324.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The funding for turnouts, PPE, firefighting tools and equipment is in the Fire
Departments baseline General Fund, Measure GG, Measure FF or UC Funds budgets.
The number of turnouts and PPE purchased each fiscal year depends on the number of
recruits hired and the number of turnouts and PPE that need to be replaced per
National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) requirements.
Firefighting tools and equipment are replaced due to normal wear and tear during fire
suppression operations.

sections.current_situation:
Turnouts and PPE are necessary protective safety gear for firefighting personnel and
are required to fully comply with updated CAL-OSHA and National Fire Protection
Association (NFPA) 1971, Standard on Protective Ensembles for Structural Fire
Fighting and Proximity Fire Fighting. Firefighting equipment is also needed for fire
suppression and responding to emergency calls.
This purchase piggybacks off two publicly solicited cooperative contracts awarded to
L.N. Curtis & Sons. These contracts are established through a formal competitive
Request for Proposal (RFP) process conducted by Sourcewell.
 Firefighter protective gear-equipment, Contract No. #010424-LNC, RFP Notice
11/09/2023, Awarded 04/22/2024, Maturity Date 03/27/2028
 Firefighting equipment, rescue tools, and accessories, Contract No. #020124-
LNC, RFP Notice 12/14/2023, Awarded 04/22/2024, Maturity Date 04/04/2028
Piggybacking off a cooperative contract is allowable and it streamlines the procurement
process, reducing administrative costs and delays associated with a local RFP.
sections.background:
Each year the Fire Department purchases turnouts and PPE for firefighters performing
in the line of duty. In addition, reliable mission critical tools and equipment are needed
for them to perform their duties. The products offered by L.N. Curtis and Sons meet
national, state and local safety standards and provide safe protective clothing and
equipment for firefighters engaging in fire suppression and rescue operations.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
PPE and equipment are required for firefighters to be maintain a state of readiness and
be compliant with national and state laws. Relying on Sourcewell’s procurement
process will minimize staff hours spent on this project while ensuring there is a
competitive process.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative actions are considered.
CONTACT PERSON
David Sprague, Fire Chief, (510) 981-3473

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ANNUAL PURCHASE ORDERS FOR L.N. CURTIS AND SONS: FIRE DEPARTMENT
WHEREAS, the Fire Department purchases turnouts and Personal Protective
Equipment (PPE) for firefighters, and
WHEREAS, reliable mission critical tools and equipment are needed for them to
perform their duties, and
WHEREAS, the products offered by L.N. Curtis and Sons meet national, state and local
safety standards that provide safe protective clothing and equipment for firefighters
engaging in fire suppression and rescue operations, and
WHEREAS, this purchase piggybacks off two publicly solicited cooperative contracts
awarded to L.N. Curtis & Sons, and
WHEREAS, these contracts are established through a formal competitive Request for
Proposal (RFP) process conducted by Sourcewell, and
WHERE AS, one is for firefighter protective gear-equipment, Contract No. #010424-
LNC, RFP Notice 11/09/2023, Awarded 04/22/2024, Maturity Date 03/27/2028, and
WHEREAS, one is for firefighting equipment, rescue tools, and accessories, Contract
No. #020124-LNC, RFP Notice 12/14/2023, Awarded 04/22/2024, Maturity Date
04/04/2028.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute annual purchase orders
and any amendments with L.N. Curtis and Sons for the purchases of turnouts, and
personal protective equipment (PPE) such as wildland coats and pants, gloves,
helmets, hearing and eye protection, fire boots, firefighting tools and equipment such as
firehose, fire extinguishers, foam, ladders, pumps, and related tools and equipment, and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the purchase order for FY2026 shall not exceed
$330,000, with an annual increase no more than 5% each year through March 27, 2028.
A 20% contingency is added to cover costs arising from unforeseen incidents or
